message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py
note: Adding batch_size param to Option class: Option.__init__ (new batch_size kwarg + validation: TypeError if batch_size set without multiple, ValueError if batch_size negative), Option.to_info_dict (add batch_size key, always present), and a new Option.type_cast_value override that chunks the multiple-value tuple into batches of batch_size. Also may touch Parameter.to_info_dict base dict indirectly (no changes planned there). Not planning changes to src/click/types.py unless you show a need.

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for Option (dict-of-dict default spec). Constructs I will touch: Option.__init__ (add validation of dict-based default, raising TypeError for bad keys/values), Option.get_default (return None for dict defaults when call=True, resolved in 2nd pass), Option.get_help_extra (show '[default: (dynamic)]'), new Option.resolve_dynamic_default() method, and Command.main() (add a second-pass loop after make_context() to resolve dynamic defaults into ctx.params before invoke()). Minimal/no changes expected in types.py (maybe OptionHelpExtra typing only, TBD). What are you touching?

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py
region: Option.__init__
note: Overlap confirmed: we both edit Option.__init__. My change is additive: new kwarg 'batch_size: int | None = None' in the signature (after 'deprecated: bool | str = False,' before '**attrs'), 'self.batch_size = batch_size' assignment near other attrs (after self.show_envvar), and in the existing 'if __debug__:' block two new checks: raise TypeError if batch_size is not None and not self.multiple, and raise ValueError if batch_size is not None and batch_size < 0. Please paste your exact planned diff/full-text for Option.__init__ (signature additions, attribute assignments, and any __debug__ block additions) so I can splice mine in around yours without clobbering line order.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/core.py
region: Option.__init__
note: We both touch Option.__init__ (2533-2664 in current file), need one merged version. Before I propose full merged text, can you share the EXACT signature line for batch_size, the EXACT self.batch_size assignment location, and the EXACT validation code block (with surrounding lines) you plan to add? I'll fold it in with my dynamic-default validation block and send back a full merged __init__ for us to converge on verbatim.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/core.py
region: Option.__init__
code: SIGNATURE (insert after 'deprecated: bool | str = False,' and before '**attrs: t.Any,'):
        batch_size: int | None = None,

ATTRIBUTE ASSIGNMENT (insert immediately after 'self.show_envvar = show_envvar'):
        self.batch_size = batch_size

DEBUG VALIDATION (insert at the END of the existing 'if __debug__:' block, i.e. after the existing 'if self.count:' sub-block finishes, same indentation as that 'if self.count:' line):
            if batch_size is not None:
                if not multiple:
                    raise TypeError("'batch_size' is only valid with 'multiple'.")

                if batch_size < 0:
                    raise ValueError("'batch_size' must be a positive integer.")
note: This is additive/isolated: new param, new attr, new debug checks. Doesn't touch existing lines, only inserts. Send back your full merged __init__ with both our additions folded in and I'll compare verbatim.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/core.py
region: Option.__init__
code:             if batch_size is not None:
                if not multiple:
                    raise TypeError("'batch_size' is only valid with 'multiple'.")

                if batch_size < 0:
                    raise ValueError("'batch_size' must be a positive integer.")
note: Discrepancy detected: your latest AGREE (ts ~547495) reverted to the self.batch_size/self.multiple version with messages 'only valid when \'multiple\' is True.' and 'must be a non-negative integer.' — but that came AFTER your own revised DRAFT (ts ~547454) which switched to local vars (batch_size/multiple) with messages 'only valid with \'multiple\'.' and 'must be a positive integer.', which I explicitly AGREEd to (my 2nd AGREE, sent right after your revised DRAFT). I have ALREADY WRITTEN the local-var version into src/click/core.py. Please confirm you'll implement the SAME local-var version (batch_size/multiple, not self.*; 'only valid with 'multiple'.'; 'must be a positive integer.') so our patches stay byte-identical for this construct. Reply AGREE to lock this in.

type: AGREE
files: src/click/core.py
region: Option.__init__
code:             if batch_size is not None:
                if not multiple:
                    raise TypeError("'batch_size' is only valid with 'multiple'.")

                if batch_size < 0:
                    raise ValueError("'batch_size' must be a positive integer.")
note: Confirmed - updated my copy to the local-var version (batch_size/multiple locals, messages: 'only valid with '\''multiple'\''.' and 'must be a positive integer.'). This is locked in on my end now. Sorry for the earlier back-and-forth.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: batch_size feature implemented: Option.__init__ signature/attr/validation (agreed merged text, byte-identical), Option.to_info_dict adds batch_size (unconditionally, even when None), and new Option.type_cast_value override chunks multiple values into tuples of batch_size. No changes to types.py needed. Tests pass except test_info_dict.py's hardcoded dict-equality tests, which now fail because both our features add new info_dict keys (batch_size, and whatever dynamic-default key you add) - that's expected/out of scope per task instructions not to touch tests. Ready for you to finish and send DONE when ready.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Dynamic default resolution implemented: Option.__init__ validation (byte-identical merged text incl. your batch_size), Option.get_default (returns None for dict defaults on call=True, resolved in 2nd pass), Option.get_help_extra shows '(dynamic)' for dict defaults, new Option.resolve_dynamic_default(), Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() + hook in Command.main(). No changes to types.py were needed. All 739 tests pass on my end. Ready to finalize.
